summary:
The main issue with using grids during radiography is generating unwanted secondary radiation fog which degrades image clarity and reduces diagnostic accuracy due to improper usage or setup issues.
The primary concern with employing grids for radiographic imaging involves creating undesirable secondary radiation fog on films; thus compromising picture sharpness and interpretability because of incorrect application techniques.
